explanation: The input requirement lists the modes that the controller must implement. The atomic propositions include mode indicators: StartUpMode (start-up), SelfTestMode (self-test), StandbyMode (VentilationOff), PCVMode (Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode), PSVMode (Pressure Support Ventilation Mode), and failSafeMode (fail-safe). The most fundamental LTL requirement corresponding to the implementation of these modes is that it should always eventually be possible to reach one of these modes (i.e., the system always offers at least one of these modes at every point in time, or the presence of these modes is guaranteed). Thus, we can use a global Eventually (GF-style, but here we use repeated F in LTL syntax for simplicity) stating that eventually, the system is in StartUpMode or SelfTestMode or StandbyMode or PCVMode or PSVMode or failSafeMode, at some time in the future. This ensures the system is always able to reach (implement) at least one of the designated modes. This is best captured by the LTL pattern for 'eventually reach some mode(s)', which is typically: F (mode1 | mode2 | ...). Here we use template 9 (F bool_exp3), where bool_exp3 is the disjunction (OR) of all the listed modes.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode

explanation:
The requirement specifies that the controller must implement several distinct modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff (Standby), Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCVMode), Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSVMode), and fail-safe mode (failSafeMode). Each of these is indicated by a dedicated atomic proposition that signals when the system is in (or has switched to) that mode. The core property implied here is that, at every moment in the system's operation, it must be in at least one of these modes (since the system must always be in some defined state according to the specification), and these are the only permissible modes. 

The most direct and succinct LTL formula that expresses 'at any time, the system is in one of these modes' is G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode). This uses template 7, G(bool_exp3), where bool_exp3 is the disjunction of all the implemented mode indicators. This property ensures that the controller always implements and correctly signals being in one of the required modes, and no time exists where the system is outside all specified modes.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode

explanation: The input requires the controller to implement several modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, Standby/VentilationOff Mode, Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCV), Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSV), and Fail-Safe Mode. Each of these modes is represented by an atomic proposition in the provided proposition set. To express that the controller must, at some point, enter each of these modes, we use the 'eventually' (F) operator for each mode, combined together with conjunction. Since the natural language simply calls for implementation/attendance of these modes (not a sequence or mutual exclusion), the most basic LTL capturing this requirement is that all these modes are eventually active at some point in system execution. Thus, for all these modes, we require F(mode) to hold. In the provided template set, template 9 (F bool_exp3) best matches the scenario—though for the AND of several eventually statements, we just AND them together using this template repeatedly. Here, we demonstrate a conjunctive composition, with bool_exp3 being the AND of all required modes. N_DURATION is not used for this template.

The natural language requirement lists the controller's major high-level modes of operation: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff, PCV, PSV, and fail-safe. Each of these modes has a directly corresponding atomic proposition. Since there is no constraint on the timing, transition, or mutual exclusion in the provided sentence, the basic property to capture is that the system can be in one of these modes at any time (i.e., these are valid/implemented states), but it does not specify what causes switching or prohibits combination. The most basic LTL property reflecting the requirement is that, globally, one of these modes is always possible. This is best captured as a disjunction of these atomic propositions always being possibly true (i.e., the system globally supports being in any of these modes). Thus, we use the template 'bool_exp3' and define bool_exp3 as the disjunction of all the relevant propositions.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode

explanation: The requirement asks for the implementation of distinct operating modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff, Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) Mode, and fail-safe. Each of these is directly mapped to atomic propositions: 'StartUpMode', 'SelfTestMode', 'StandbyMode' (aka VentilationOff), 'PCVMode', 'PSVMode', and 'failSafeMode', all of which indicate when their respective modes are active. To capture the requirement that these modes must be implemented, we can specify that (at all times) the system will always be in at least one of these six modes (i.e., at any instant, one or more must be true). The most basic LTL template that captures this property is to globally require that at least one of these mode-indicating propositions holds: G (StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode). This means the system always is in one (or more) of the listed modes, which is the implementation of the mode functionality as described. This does not enforce mutual exclusion or transitions, but as a direct encoding of the 'modes are implemented and present', it's the most likely interpretation given the natural language input.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode

explanation: The input natural language specifies that the controller shall implement six distinct operating modes, each of which has a corresponding atomic proposition indicating when that mode is active (StartUpMode, SelfTestMode, StandbyMode for VentilationOff, PCVMode for Pressure Controlled Ventilation, PSVMode for Pressure Support Ventilation, and failSafeMode for fail-safe). The most basic requirement is that each of these modes is implemented, i.e., each can possibly occur (become true) at some point during operation, which corresponds to requesting that eventually each of these atomic propositions holds (F atomic_proposition). To capture this for all modes, we can use a disjunction of eventualities, but since the strictest interpretation of 'shall implement the following modes' is that all must be possible (not just some), a conjunction over each F(mode) is required. Since the LTL template list does not have a direct conjunction of multiple eventualities, the best fitting template is template 9 ('F bool_exp3'), and we set bool_exp3 to a conjunction (AND) over all six atomic propositions. This expresses that at some point, all six modes will have been active (possibly at different times, but each must become true at least once) – thus, they are implemented by the controller. N_DURATION is not required for this template, so its value is null.

The input describes a requirement for the controller to implement (support) a set of operational modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff (Standby), Pressure Controlled Ventilation, Pressure Support Ventilation, and Fail-safe. Each of these modes is represented by an atomic proposition: StartUpMode, SelfTestMode, StandbyMode (aka VentilationOff), PCVMode, PSVMode, and failSafeMode. At any time, the system is considered to be implementing these modes if at least one of the atomic propositions corresponding to those modes is true. The simplest way to capture the requirement that 'the controller shall implement' these modes (i.e., at any time, the system is in one of these modes) is to use a global (G) LTL operator asserting that at every moment, the system is in at least one of the required modes. Therefore, the correct template is the 'Globally' (G) template, and the boolean expression is a disjunction (logical OR) of all the listed mode propositions.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode

explanation: The natural language requirement is that the controller must implement specific operational modes, including Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff, Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode (PCV), Pressure Support Ventilation Mode (PSV), and fail-safe mode. Each of these operational modes is directly referenced by a corresponding atomic proposition. The strongest property guaranteed by this requirement is that, at some point during the controller's operation, it must reach a state corresponding to each of these modes. Thus, the most appropriate LTL translation is a formula stating that, eventually, each one of these mode-indicating atomic propositions becomes true at least once during execution. This is captured by logically OR-ing the 'Finally' properties for each atomic proposition, or more compactly as a single 'Finally' expression over the OR of all the atomic proposition variables. This ensures that the controller implements (i.e., is able to enter) each required mode at some point, satisfying the requirement stated in the input.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode

explanation: The requirement states that the controller shall implement six specific operational modes: Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff (Standby), Pressure Controlled Ventilation Mode, Pressure Support Ventilation Mode, and fail-safe. Therefore, the specification should guarantee that, at all times, the system is in at least one of these modes. To ensure that the system always remains in one (and only in one, but the requirement only mentions implementation, not mutual exclusivity), a strong interpretation is to state that globally (at every time), at least one of these mode indicators is true. Thus, we use the LTL template G bool_exp3, where 'bool_exp3' is the disjunction of all six mode atomic propositions. This expresses that at all times, the controller is in some implemented mode as required.

The natural language requirement states the controller shall implement various operation modes, including Start-up Mode, Self-Test Mode, VentilationOff (alias StandbyMode), Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV), Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV), and a fail-safe mode. Since this is a global/static requirement that these modes be available or implemented, the best mapping is that it is always (globally) the case (G) that it must be possible to reach one of these modes: StartUpMode, SelfTestMode, StandbyMode, PCVMode, PSVMode, failSafeMode. Using boolean OR between all the mode atomic propositions captures the idea that at any point, the system can indicate it is in at least one of these defined modes. This translation chooses 'G (bool_exp3)' (template 7) with bool_exp3 as the disjunction (OR) of these mode indicators.
bool_exp3: StartUpMode | SelfTestMode | StandbyMode | PCVMode | PSVMode | failSafeMode
